title: Skopeo now requires capabilities when executed as regular user
body:
It uses to work fine in bubblewrap to copy image between registry but it no longer do:

```
$ ./skopeo --version
skopeo version 0.1.36-dev commit: 904b064da44eecfeea57f2c935e230fd41574ac8
$ bwrap  --unshare-all --bind / / --proc /proc --dev dev /bin/bash
$ ./skopeo copy docker-daemon:test:latest docker-daemon:test:test
Error: error writing "allow" to setgroups: write /proc/33/setgroups: operation not permitted
ERRO[0000] error opening "allow" to /proc/33/setgroups: write /proc/33/setgroups: operation not permitted 
ERRO[0000] (unable to determine exit status)   

$ bwrap --cap-add CAP_CHOWN --cap-add CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE --cap-add CAP_FOWNER --cap-add CAP_FSETID --cap-add CAP_MKNOD --cap-add CAP_SETFCAP --unshare-all --bind / / --proc /proc --dev dev /bin/bash
$ ./skopeo copy docker-daemon:test:latest docker-daemon:test:test
OK
```

Shouldn't the tool checks if the capabilities are really needed before requiring them (e.g. when doing remote copies)?

comments.body: we could treat this case separately, but I think it is safer to require these capabilities as we have not really checked out we never use `chown` when running as an unprivileged user.
Skopeo should not be attempting to enter a user namespace unless it is using container-storage.
